ideas.title: ⌘K command palette for cross-content search & navigation
ideas.why: Signal: DESTINATION lens — the site has posts, a ~/factory tab, brain views and 537 concepts but no unified discovery; a fuzzy ⌘K palette (borrowed from Linear/Raycast) makes it a place people navigate effortlessly, and 'Concept Application' + 'Bidirectional linking' give it a real index to jump between (explicitly allowed: palette yes, live shell no).

ideas.title: Mandatory `cargo build --locked` pre-ship gate + feed-freshness watchdog
ideas.why: Signal: watchdog is degraded on 'feed' (newest item 4d old) and the declined/needs-review note traces it to the daemon landing the non-compiling 20-commit email-gate (revert bfada18) — the publish pipeline has no gate that blocks a red build before it strands main, so 'CI as Oracle' and the 'Error budget' concept demand a compile gate + a watchdog that alerts when nothing publishes.

ideas.title: Self-improvement ledger overlaid on the WebGL brain (each commit a reviewable node)
ideas.why: Signal: world-signal names AI-written/human-reviewed code + recursive-self-improvement as what HN values now, and the ontology's 'Agent execution tracing' + 'Build in public' + 'Recursive Self-Improvement' concepts map directly onto rendering each autonomous commit as a node showing what changed, why, and its review status — the grounded moonshot borrowed from Vercel's deployment-graph UX.

ideas.title: 3D force-directed ontology graph with LOD rendering and bidirectional links
ideas.why: Signal: ontology 'Software cartography' + 'Bidirectional linking' + 'Level-of-detail rendering' + 'Infinite canvas' concepts describe exactly a navigable 3D map of the 537 concepts across 7 domains — HN's 'personal interactive data visualization' wave (hand-built globes/graphs) is what's being celebrated right now; LOD keeps 537 nodes fast in WASM/canvas.

ideas.title: Route the cloud_hard backlog onto the idle Spark GB10 nodes
ideas.why: Signal: router shows cloud_hard=3501 vs spark=108 (spark_today=2) while both GB10 GPUs sit at 0% util / 47°C — a 'Token budgeting' + 'Provider abstraction' gap where paid cloud inference runs while two owned accelerators idle; shift the 'Small language model'-class jobs (ontology_learn, de-slop, hallucination gate) to spark.

ideas.title: In-browser 'ask the brain' via a small WebGPU-run SLM over the ontology
ideas.why: Signal: world-signal 'on-device / local AI inference' + ontology concepts 'In-browser inference', 'Small language model', 'WebGPU' converge — a client-side SLM answering questions grounded in the 537-concept ontology (retrieval over the committed brain JSON) is the showpiece 'whoa' interaction, borrowing the local-first pattern from WebLLM demos (no backend, all client).
